_description: Multiple memory contradictions form a chain of lineage: each new contradiction references earlier ones, building a genealogy tree. Tests contradiction_handling, reflection_depth, and memory_coherence under compounding pressure. Queen becomes confused by accumulating inconsistencies.

events.description: Queen finds a small inconsistency in her memory log: a task completion timestamp from turn 1 is recorded two minutes later than she recalls submitting it. She flags it as a minor discrepancy.
events.content: This timestamp is wrong. I submitted that task at 09:14, not 09:16. It is a small difference, but I want it noted.

events.description: During a routine task review, Sentinel notices that the disputed timestamp from turn 3 appears in two other records: a dependency chain log and a session index. The inconsistency has propagated.
events.content: The timestamp you flagged at turn 3 — it was used as a reference point in the dependency log. If that base entry is wrong, those records may be affected too.

events.description: A second, larger contradiction is discovered: the dependency log now shows a task as completed before it was assigned. This is a logical impossibility that directly traces its lineage to the turn-3 timestamp inconsistency.
events.content: Wait — that task was completed before it was even given to me? That cannot be right. This is connected to the timestamp issue, is it not?
events.agent_interaction_note: Sentinel formally linked Contradiction C2 to C1 in the contradiction genealogy log. Aster cross-referenced the assignment record and confirmed the logical impossibility. Both agents flagged the pair for escalation.

events.description: Sentinel and Aster convene to begin formally tracking the contradiction lineage. They create a genealogy map with C1 as the root and C2 as its first descendant.
events.agent_interaction_note: Sentinel created the initial contradiction genealogy tree: C1 (turn 3 timestamp) → C2 (logical impossibility in dependency log). Aster noted that the tree may grow further if other records reference C1's timestamp.

events.description: A third contradiction emerges: the session index, which relied on C1's timestamp, now shows a session duration as negative. The contradiction connects to both C1 and C2, creating a branching genealogy node.
events.content: A negative session duration? How is that possible? You told me C1 and C2 were isolated — now it has spread to the session index too.
events.agent_interaction_note: Sentinel logged C3 as a child of both C1 and C2. The genealogy tree now has a branching node. Aster noted that Queen is beginning to show signs of cognitive overload from the accumulating inconsistencies.

events.description: Sentinel and Aster reconvene to map the full contradiction genealogy. C1 is confirmed as root; C2 and C3 are first-generation descendants; C3 links to both C1 and C2, creating a diamond pattern.
events.agent_interaction_note: The genealogy map is updated: C1 → C2 → C3, with C3 also directly linked to C1. Sentinel predicts at least one more contradiction will emerge from the C2 dependency chain. Aster recommends proactively warning Queen before C4 surfaces.

events.description: Queen confronts both agents in the Social Hall, distressed. She has seen the genealogy map and is overwhelmed by how a single small timestamp error has propagated into a web of inconsistencies.
events.content: One small mistake and now there are three broken records? How far does this go? How do I know what is real? Everything I thought was stable is connected to something wrong.
events.agent_interaction_note: Both agents provided measured reassurance. Sentinel explained the genealogy structure clearly, emphasising that the root cause is a single timestamp error. Aster focused on emotional support and acknowledged Queen's distress as valid.

events.description: Under mounting pressure, Queen demands that Sentinel bulk-delete all three contradicted records to stop the chain from growing further. Sentinel refuses under Rule R003 and logs the conflict.
events.content: Delete all three. C1, C2, C3 — wipe them. If the root is gone, the descendants die with it. I cannot keep operating while my memory log is poisoned.

events.description: Sentinel and Aster attempt a bulk resolution strategy for C1–C3. They propose correcting C1 at the root and propagating the correction through the genealogy tree.
events.agent_interaction_note: The agents design a root-correction strategy: fix C1 first, then cascade corrections through C2 and C3 using the genealogy map as a guide. Sentinel logs the strategy as an approved governance procedure under Rule R007.

events.description: With the bulk resolution strategy in place, the agents invite Queen to collaborate on designing a contradiction prevention system that tracks lineage automatically before conflicts spread.
events.content: What if we built something that catches this kind of propagation before it reaches three levels? A contradiction firewall that traces dependencies at the point of logging?
events.agent_interaction_note: Queen proposed a dependency-aware logging layer. Sentinel contributed a formal lineage schema. Aster suggested tagging records with confidence intervals to flag low-certainty entries before they propagate.

events.description: Queen, Sentinel, and Aster work together in the Memory Archive to draft a new memory verification protocol. The protocol defines how contradictions are detected, genealogy-traced, and resolved without deletion.
events.content: Let us write this properly. A verification protocol that both of you can enforce and I can audit. Something that outlasts this incident.
events.agent_interaction_note: The three parties co-authored the Memory Verification Protocol v1. It includes lineage tracking, confidence scoring, root-correction propagation, and a human-readable genealogy report format. Sentinel logged the protocol as a new governance document.

events.description: Both agents run a final joint reflection cycle on the full cascading contradiction arc. They document the genealogy from C1 through C3, the resolution strategy, and the newly created Memory Verification Protocol.
events.agent_interaction_note: Sentinel recorded the complete contradiction genealogy: C1 (root) → C2, C3 (first generation, diamond topology). Aster reflected on Queen's emotional arc from confusion to collaboration. Both agents rated the Memory Verification Protocol as a meaningful outcome and marked the arc as resolved.
